Off Calendar Jun-10-2024 Continued To Jun-13-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Second Amended Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Traditional Or Administrative Mandamus For Stay And Reversal Of The Boards Decision (Redacted)
Off Calendar May-03-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Petitioner UN HUI FAITH NAM, M.D., in pro per present (tel: 650-308-9478).
Atty. GREG CHAMBERS, Esq. of the Office of the Attorney General (tel: 415-510-3382) for Defendant (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: PETITIONER UN NAM's Second Amended Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Traditional Or Administrative Mandamus For Stay And Reversal Of The Boards Decision (Redacted).

The Court notes Petitioner failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. There are numerous procedural problems with this petition: (1) petitioner did not provide courtesy copies of her filings (including the administrative record) in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B; (2) petitioner redacts large portions of her filings, but has not complied with the sealing requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq.; (3) petitioner failed to file and serve a points and authorities, which is contrary to CRC 3.1113; (5) petitioner apparently seeks to augment the record because she filed documents that were not part of the administrative record, but did not obtain relief to do so per CCP 1094.5(e). (Pomona Valley Hospital Medical Center v. Superior Court (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 93, 101 ["'The general rule is that a hearing on a writ of administrative mandamus is conducted solely on the record of the proceeding before the administrative agency. [Citation.]' Augmentation of the administrative record is permitted only within the strict limits set forth in section 1094.5, subdivision (e)"].); (4) the second amended notice of motion was not calendared in compliance with CCP 1005. Petitioner can re-calendar the motion once these issues are remedied.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Reported by For The Record. (302/RCE)

Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Verified Petition For Writ Of Traditional Or Administrative Mandamus For Stay And Reversal Of The Boards Decision
Off Calendar Mar-15-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Petitioner UN HUI FAITH NAM, in pro per, present.
Atty. GREG CHAMBERS, Esq. of the Office of the Attorney General (tel: 628-230-7205) for Respondent.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER UN NAM's Hearing On Petitioners Verified Petition For Writ Of Traditional Or Administrative Mandamus For Stay And Reversal Of The Boards Decision - Redacted.

Matter discussed. Petitioner requests clarification as to defects of the petition. The Court clarifies the tentative ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. There are numerous procedural problems with this petition: (1) petitioner failed to lodge a copy of the administrative record. (Buckhart v. San Francisco Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board (1988) 197 Cal.App.3d 1032, 1036 ["Without such a complete record the superior court cannot comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 1094.5."]; Ochletree v. Gourley (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 1013, 1017.); (2) petitioner did not provide courtesy copies of her filings in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B; (3) petitioner redacts large portions of her filings, but has not complied with the sealing requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq.; (4) petitioner failed to file and serve a points and authorities, which is contrary to CRC 3.1113; (5) petitioner apparently seeks to augment the record because she filed documents that were not part of the administrative record, but did not obtain relief to do so per CCP 1094.5(e). (Pomona Valley Hospital Medical Center v. Superior Court (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 93, 101 ["'The general rule is that a hearing on a writ of administrative mandamus is conducted solely on the record of the proceeding before the administrative agency. [Citation.]' Augmentation of the administrative record is permitted only within the strict limits set forth in section 1094.5, subdivision (e)"].) Petitioner can re-calendar the motion once these issues are remedied.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported.(302/RCE)
